7-maths-ch1.label: Ch 1: Large Number Around Us
7-maths-ch1.title: Class 7 Maths: Ch 1 - Large Numbers Around Us

questions.q: Find the value of the expression: 30 + 5 × 4
questions.explain: Multiplication is done first: 5 × 4 = 20, then 30 + 20 = 50. Without brackets, we evaluate the term 5 × 4 first before adding.

questions.q: What are the terms of the expression 83 – 14?
questions.explain: Subtracting a number is the same as adding its inverse: 83 – 14 = 83 + (−14). So the terms are 83 and −14.

questions.q: "Swapping the terms of an expression does not change its value" is called the:
questions.explain: This is the commutative property of addition, e.g., 6 + (−4) = (−4) + 6.

questions.q:
Irfan paid ₹100 for a biscuit packet (₹15) and toor dal (₹56). What is the change he gets?
Expression: 100 – (15 + 56)
questions.explain: First evaluate the bracket: 15 + 56 = 71. Then 100 – 71 = 29. Irfan gets ₹29 as change.

questions.q: When brackets preceded by a negative sign are removed, the signs of the terms inside the brackets:
questions.explain: For example, 100 – (15 + 56) = 100 – 15 – 56. Both +15 and +56 change sign to −15 and −56 once the bracket is removed.
First subtract inside the bracket: 250 – 100 = 150. Then 500 – 150 = 350. Note: this is NOT the same as 500 – 250 – 100.

questions.q: Using the distributive property, 14 × 10 – 6 × 10 can be written as:
questions.explain: The multiple of a difference is the same as the difference of the multiples: 14 × 10 – 6 × 10 = (14 – 6) × 10 = 8 × 10 = 80.

questions.q:
Hira has 28 coins in one bag and 35 in another. She gifts 10 coins from the second bag. Find the coins left:
28 + (35 – 10)
questions.explain: Since the bracket is not preceded by a negative sign, signs don't change: 28 + 35 – 10 = 63 – 10 = 53.
97 × 25 = 100 × 25 – 3 × 25 = 2500 – 75 = 2425. This distributive trick makes mental multiplication faster.
